category: 23: Construction / 2373: Highway, Street, and Bridge Construction
description: This dataset represents the operation of pavers, diesel powered using nonroad diesel as a fuel source. Emissions and fuel‑consumption data are calculated using EPA’s Motor Vehicle Emission Simulator (MOVES),  the regulatory model used to determine emission inventories for equipment used in construction, agriculture, industrial, and commercial sectors. The reference flows are: 1. the operation of equipment providing 1 MJ of energy; and 2. the operation of equipment for 1 hour.

flow.category: Technosphere Flows / 23: Construction / 2373: Highway, Street, and Bridge Construction
flow.name: Operation of equipment; pavers

flow.category: Technosphere flows/44-45: Retail Trade/4471: Gasoline Stations
flow.name: Diesel; dispensed at pump

name: Operation of equipment; pavers; diesel powered

processDocumentation.completenessDescription: The results include emissions from running exhaust, crankcase venting, refueling vapor and spillage,  evaporative tank permeation, evaporative hose permeation, and fuel vapor venting from diurnal, hot soak, and running activity. Evaporative emissions while the equipment is soaking (engine off) are not included. Manufacturing of equipment, roads, and other infrastructure are not included in this dataset. The model for nonroad equipment does not include nitrous oxide (N2O).
processDocumentation.dataCollectionDescription: MOVES runs were performed using MOVES4.0.1 in 2025.
processDocumentation.dataSelectionDescription: MOVES runs were performed at the default settings at the state level.
processDocumentation.dataTreatmentDescription: Emissions are calculated using an adjusted emission factor multiplied by rated power, load factor, engine population, and activity. The model uses base-year equipment populations by technology type and model year, and applies growth factors to estimate the population in the analysis year. Nonroad emissions by equipment type, fuel type, and age are estimated based on median life at full load, load factors, activity, and age distributions. Equipment populations are allocated to the county level using surrogate data.
processDocumentation.geographyDescription: This process represents emissions from equipment operated in all U.S. states.
processDocumentation.intendedApplication: The target audience of this model includes LCA practitioners, industry, and the general public.  A full inventory of environmental flows are included;  thus this unit process can be used for a full range of LCIA impact categories.
processDocumentation.projectDescription: This dataset was developed by the Federal LCA Commons Data Curator Eastern Research Group, Inc. (ERG) under subcontract (SUB-2024-10066) to the National Renewable Energy Laboratory under Department of Energy (DOE) Prime Contract No. DE-AC36-08GO28308. The work is funded by DOE's Advanced Materials & Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO).

processDocumentation.samplingDescription: Emissions and activity data for each state are combined to generate an activity-weighted average for each region. No uncertainty was assessed.
processDocumentation.technologyDescription: This process reflects the nonroad diesel-powered pavers, diesel powered used in the construction sector, with an average horsepower of 1.2e+02 hp and a load factor of 0.59.
processDocumentation.timeDescription: This process reflects the equipment as estimated in 2020. MOVES estimates daily emissions for weekdays and  weekends by month and year for calendar years 1990 and 1999 through 2060.
processDocumentation.useAdvice: This process has two reference units: MJ of energy delivered by the equipment, hour of equipment use.
